headline: Shares of U.S.-listed foreign banks are trading higher amid subdued energy prices and inflation concerns after President Trump suspended U.S. strikes on Iranian power generation and energy assets for five days.
summary: Shares of U.S.-listed foreign banks are trading higher amid subdued energy prices and inflation concerns after President Trump suspended U.S. strikes on Iranian power generation and energy assets for five days.
